global.description: Allows the chart to be used as a sub chart, in which case the main charts settings is made available in this object.

ingress.description: Injected by ApplicationSet — not used by this chart

managementPolicies.description: Controls whether Crossplane manages the full lifecycle of the resources or only observes them. Applies to S3 bucket resources, the IAM Policy and the RolePolicyAttachment.

deletionPolicy.description: Controls what happens when resources are deleted from Kubernetes. 'Delete' attempts to remove the AWS resource (S3 buckets cannot be deleted when non-empty). 'Orphan' leaves the AWS resource in place.

buckets.description: List of S3 buckets to manage. Each bucket can grant the namespace S3 access role (idp_ns_s3_access-<namespace>) read or write permissions via the 'access' setting and can grant namespaced deployment IRSA roles access via serviceAccountReadRoles/serviceAccountWriteRoles.

name.description: The name of the bucket. Must be a valid Kubernetes resource name (DNS-1123 label): lowercase alphanumeric and '-', 3-63 chars.

region.description: The AWS region of the bucket.

publicRead.description: Expose objects publicly via BucketPolicy (GetObject on bucket/*). Requires PublicAccessBlock.blockPublicPolicy=false.

access.description: Access level to grant the namespace S3 access role (idp_ns_s3_access-<namespace>). 'none' = no access, 'read' = s3:GetObject + s3:ListBucket, 'write' = read permissions plus s3:PutObject + s3:DeleteObject.

serviceAccountReadRoles.description: List of deployment names whose namespaced IRSA role should receive a read policy (s3:GetObject + s3:ListBucket) for this bucket. Each value resolves to <deployment>-<namespace>. If a value already ends with -<namespace>, it is used as-is.

serviceAccountWriteRoles.description: List of deployment names whose namespaced IRSA role should receive a read/write policy (s3:GetObject + s3:ListBucket + s3:PutObject + s3:DeleteObject) for this bucket. Each value resolves to <deployment>-<namespace>. If a value already ends with -<namespace>, it is used as-is. Write implies read on the same role.

tags.description: Additional tags to set on the bucket
